confirmed.rules: Daily limit: 5. Minimum size: 14 inches (with exceptions for 2 fish in some waters).
Daily limit: 20. Minimum size: 8 inches in managed Piedmont reservoirs.
Daily limit: 20. Minimum size: 8 inches in managed Piedmont reservoirs.
Generally no bag or size limits on state waters. Local park and management area rules may apply.
No state-wide limits. Often considered invasive in some regions, check local advisories.
Generally no bag or size limits. Highly migratory, check local river specific rules.
No size limit. Combined sunfish daily limit: 30 fish total.
General Hatchery Supported: 7 fish per day, no size limit. Single-hook artificial only in wild waters.
General Hatchery Supported: 7 fish per day, no size limit.
Daily limit: 8 fish. No minimum size limit (except in specific reservoirs).
Inland Waters: 4 fish per day, 20-inch minimum size.
Often follows same rules as Largemouth Bass in many states, check local limits.
Typically highly regulated. Common: 1 fish per day, 40-inch minimum size. Check local seasonal closures.
Check local region. Often 2-5 fish per day with slot limits (e.g., none kept between 22-26 inches).
Commonly no size limit, 25-50 fish daily limit in managed northern lakes.
No limit. Considered invasive/rough fish in most US states. Bowfishing allowed in most areas.
No limit in most regions. Often classified under general 'Sunfish/Panfish' aggregate limits.
Aggregate panfish limits apply (often 20-50 fish depending on state).
